title: SQL Formatter Table Stats
description: Hand-populated table/column statistics for a single data source, consumed by the query advisor (`sql-format advise`). Never collected automatically — no live DB connection is ever made by this tool. Populate by running the SQL printed by `sql-format advise stats-queries --dialect <dialect>` against your database and pasting the JSON result back in.

id.description: Stable identifier for this data source, e.g. "analytics-prod".

collectedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when these stats were collected — a hand-maintained snapshot, not live, so this tells you how stale it might be.

tables.description: Keyed by table name (unqualified, or schema-qualified if you need to disambiguate — must match how the table is referenced in the SQL you're advising on).

columns.description: Keyed by column name. Optional per column — omit what you don't know.
